channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Keep voting, election ends Sunday morning, NBA tells members
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Nigerian Bar Association reminds members to continue voting in the 2026 national election, which closes Sunday morning. Don’t miss your chance to vote!
Read More: https://punchng.com/keep-voting-election-ends-sunday-morning-nba-tells-members/

item.title: Rubio urges Cuba to release 700 political prisoners
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
US Secretary Marco Rubio calls for Cuba to immediately release over 700 political prisoners, highlighting human rights abuses by the Cuban regime.
Read More: https://punchng.com/rubio-urges-cuba-to-release-700-political-prisoners/

item.title: Adeleke, Oyebamiji present as APC secretary holds prayer for late mum
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Osun Governor Adeleke and APC candidate Oyebamiji attended a Fidau prayer, setting aside political differences. Read about their rare public interaction.
Read More: https://punchng.com/adeleke-oyebamiji-present-as-apc-secretary-holds-prayer-for-late-mum/

item.title: Littler kicks off World Matchplay defence in stunning fashion
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Luke Littler fired in an average of over 109 to kick off his World Matchplay defence in empathic fashion on Saturday.

item.title: Rate England and France players in third-place play-off
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Rate the England and France players out of 10 below and come back 30 minutes after full-time to see the final ratings.

item.title: NBA Election: Badejo-Okusanya extends lead in presidential race
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Of the three candidates, only Mrs Badejo-Okusanya secured additional votes that were substantial enough to increase her share of the total votes cast. The post NBA Election: Badejo-Okusanya extends lead in presidential race appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Soludo’s wife celebrates discharge of brutalised 10-year-old boy from Anambra hospital
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Anambra First Lady, Nonye Soludo, celebrates the full recovery and discharge of a 10-year-old boy brutalised by guardians, pledging continued support.
Read More: https://punchng.com/soludos-wife-celebrates-discharge-of-brutalised-10-year-old-boy-from-anambra-hospital/

item.title: Jigawa Suspends ₦1.3bn Hospital Renovation Contract Over Substandard Work
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Jigawa State governor, Malam Umar Namadi has ordered the immediate suspension of the ₦1.3 billion renovation and remodelling contract for Gumel General Hospital, citing poor workmanship, substandard quality, and unsatisfactory progress by the contractor. The suspension of the contract followed an un

item.title: NBA Election Not Suspended, Cancelled – President
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The President of the Nigerian Bar Association, Mazi Afam Osigwe, SAN, has said the rumoured cancelation of the election was the handy work of his detractors. According to him, the election has been fair, credible and peaceful. He said at about 5pm Saturday, over 152 lawyers have exercised their fran

item.title: TMO drama in chaotic finale as 13-man England hold on for win in Argentina
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England's rugby players avenged defeat to Argentina in the World Cup semi-finals with a chaotic, ill-tempered 31-24 Nations Championship victory that went to the wire in Santiago del Estero.

item.title: Malami’s prosecution politically motivated – Kebbi ADC
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kebbi ADC alleges Abubakar Malami’s prosecution is politically motivated, accusing the APC of using state institutions to weaken opposition ahead of 2027 e
Read More: https://punchng.com/malamis-prosecution-politically-motivated-kebbi-adc/

item.title: Atiku Slams Tinubu Over Alleged ₦210bn Budget Duplication, Demands Forensic Probe
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Vice President and presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Atiku Abubakar, has accused the President Bola Tinubu administration of fiscal recklessness following reports of alleged duplicated and overlapping allocations exceeding ₦210 billion in the 2026 federal budget

item.title: Jonathan commiserates with Amaechi over mother’s death
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Former President Goodluck Jonathan has expressed sadness over the passing of Dame Mary Amaechi, mother of a former Governor of Rivers State, Rotimi Amaechi. The post Jonathan commiserates with Amaechi over mother’s death appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Stakeholders call for stronger investment in parenting support to protect children, strengthen families
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The call was made during a stakeholder dissemination workshop titled "From the Airwaves to Stronger Families: Evaluating Parenting 101 with Isang and Shaping the Future of Parenting Support in Akwa Ibom State," The post Stakeholders call for stronger investment in parenting support to protect childr

item.title: Troops Dismantle Suspected Illegal Bunkering Site, Arrest 6 In Lagos
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army’s 65 Battalion have dismantled a suspected illegal bunkering site in the Orile General Area of Lagos State, arresting six suspects and recovering vehicles and petroleum products believed to be linked to the illicit operation. The operation, carried out on July 16, f

item.title: Lagos bunkering site dismantled as army seizes vehicles, petroleum products
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “The suspects and recovered items were subsequently handed over to the Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC) Lagos State Command by the Commanding Officer, 65 Battalion, Lieutenant Colonel S Adeojo, for further investigation and necessary legal action.”

item.title: Police Nab Fake Pastor, Accomplice Over Abduction Of 4-Week-Old Baby In Niger
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Niger State Police Command has arrested a suspected fake pastor and his alleged accomplice for the abduction of a four-week-old baby in Minna, the state capital. The Command’s Public Relations Officer, SP Wasiu Abiodun, disclosed this in a statement issued on Saturday, saying the baby has

item.title: Nigerian Rocket Ajayi blasts 9.84s to stun London Diamond League
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Auburn University product and Nigerian record holder clocked a wind-assisted but legal 9.84 seconds (-0.7 m/s) to edge out Jamaica’s reigning world champion Oblique Seville, who finished second in 9.87 seconds.

item.title: Northants beat Hampshire in thriller to win first Blast title in a decade
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: ​​​​​​​Northamptonshire claimed their first Vitality Blast title in a decade and third overall with a thrilling 14-run win over Hampshire in Saturday's see-saw Edgbaston final.

item.title: 2 US Troops Killed, 1 Missing After Iranian Missile, Drone Attack In Jordan
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Two United States service members have been killed and another declared missing following an Iranian missile and drone attack on a military installation in Jordan, the US Central Command (CENTCOM) confirmed on Saturday. The incident marks the first American military fatalities attributed to Iranian

item.title: Hydration breaks not popular and Fifa will review - Wenger
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Arsene Wenger says he accepts the hydration breaks introduced at the 2026 World Cup have not been popular and Fifa will review their impact after the tournament.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Raphael Onyedika is nearing a permanent transfer to Bundesliga club Eintracht Frankfurt after agreeing personal terms, DAILY POST reports. Eintracht Frankfurt are, however, still in talks with Belgian Pro League champions Club Brugge to finalise the deal. The Eagles have made attempts to sign Onyedi

item.title: Iran Threatens ‘Unforgettable Lessons’, As Two US Troops Killed
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: A month after the foes signed a now-abandoned preliminary deal aimed at ending their war, Tehran struck infrastructure around the Gulf in retaliation for a week of intensifying US attacks, which Iran said had hit an airport, a railway station, and bridges. The post Iran Threatens ‘Unforgettabl

item.title: NPFL: Kabiru Dogo begins work at Niger Tornadoes
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Kabiru Dogo has resumed duties as technical adviser of Niger Tornadoes, DAILY POST reports. Dogo was appointed to his new role earlier this month. He had his first training session with the team on Saturday. The experienced tactician guided El-Kanemi Warriors to the 2026 President Federation Cup tri

item.title: Oyo APC crisis deepens as six governorship aspirants reject Sharafadeen Alli’s candidacy
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: A fresh crisis has emerged within the Oyo State chapter of the All Progressives Congress (APC) after six of the party's 11 governorship aspirants rejected the emergence of Senator Sharafadeen Alli as the party's gubernatorial candidate for the 2027 general election. item.description: Today marks not merely the passage of another year, but a commemoration of the profound and lasting impact of a leader whose political philosophy and personal conduct have fundamentally reshaped the definition of leadership in contemporary Nigeria. item.description: Iran and the US have traded strikes as hostilities re-escalated this week, with Tehran targeting US military facilities across the Gulf region.

item.title: NPFL: Akin Maxwell appointed Doma United sporting director
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Akin Maxwell has been appointed the new Sporting Director of Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) side Doma United. Maxwell previously served as the Chairman of Osun United. He has also worked with NPFL side Shooting Stars. Maxwell’s track record is expected to help the Savannah Tigers on th

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President of the Nigeria Tennis Federation (NTF), Rt. Hon. Victor Ochei, has hailed the historic achievements of Nigeria’s men’s and women’s national tennis teams after both secured promotion in their respective international competitions, describing 2026 as a landmark year for the sport. Nigeria’s

item.title: World Cup: Iniesta advises Spain on how to beat Messi-led Argentina

item.description: Former Spain international, Andres Iniesta, has advised the La Roja on how to beat Lionel Messi-led Argentina when both sides clash in the World Cup final on Sunday. Iniesta also warned Spain that it would be completely impossible to shut Messi down during the encounter. Speaking to Tribal Football,

item.title: Latin America unites to cheer against Argentina in FIFA World Cup final
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Fans from Mexico to Brazil unite to cheer Yamal's Spain against Argentina and Messi, whom they call 'FIFA’s golden boy'.

item.title: DLA appoints ex-NNPP chairman, Odeyemi as acting Osun chair
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Former Osun State Chairman of the New Nigeria Peoples Party (NNPP), Dr. Tosin Odeyemi, has been appointed Acting Chairman of the Democratic Leadership Alliance (DLA) in the state. The appointment, which takes immediate effect, was conveyed in a letter dated July 17, 2026, and signed by the National

item.title: Manchester United lose to Wrexham in first pre-season match
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A strong Manchester United XI was defeated by Wrexham 1-0 in its first match of the pre-season at the Olympic stadium in Helsinki, Finland. The Welsh side playing in the English Championship scored in the winning goal in the first half against their more illustrious English Premier League opponents

item.title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. The man has written countless novels, screenplays, pieces of journalism, started McSweeney's, and founded multiple schools and nonprofits that support writers and the arts more broadly. So one might expect

item.description: France and England will battle for the 2026 FIFA World Cup bronze medal today, Saturday, July 18, as both sides attempt to end disappointing semifinal exits with a consolation victory. Okonkwo, 24, has now kept clean sheets in back-to-back pre-season matches, after Wrexham played goalless with

item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Severe thunderstorms in New Jersey have forced Spain to cancel their final outdoor training session at Melanie Lane Training Ground ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final against Argentina. Under tournament protocol, training must be postponed if lightning is detected within an eight-mile radius. Li

item.title: “Kidnappers Washed Our Clothes When We Started Smelling” — Rescued Oyo Teacher Explains Why They Appeared Clean After Rescue

item.description: A teacher rescued from the Oriire school abduction in Oyo State, Zachery Olatunde, has dismissed claims that the kidnapping was staged, revealing that the abductors occasionally washed the captives’ clothes during their 56 days in captivity. Speaking in a video shared by Oyo Matters, Olatunde said t

Ademola Adeleke of the Accord Party, and the All Progressives Congress (APC) governorship candidate, Asiwaju Munirudeen Bola Oyebamiji (AMBO), shared a rare moment of cordiality during the fidau prayer of late Alhaja S

item.description: Chelsea have agreed a record £117m deal with Aston Villa for Morgan Rogers. The Blues are now on the brink of hijacking Arsenal’s move to sign the England midfielder. The Gunners were unwilling to go high price and Rogers is set to join Chelsea. Rogers is expected to undergo his medicals on Mo

According to a statement issued on Saturday by the P

item.title: NBA Election: Over 22,000 Lawyers Vote As Turnout Reaches 27.56%
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Voting in the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) presidential election continued on Saturday evening, with more than 22,000 lawyers casting their ballots as voter turnout climbed to 27.56 per cent. The latest update released at 6:52 p.m. showed that 22,645 lawyers had voted out of 82,164 eligible voters

item.title: Abbas Distributes 60,000 Bags of Fertiliser To Constituents

item.description: The Speaker of the House of Representatives, Hon. Tajudeen Abbas, distributed 60,000 bags of fertiliser among various segments of his constituents on Saturday. Abbas also extended this gesture to the seven other local governments within the Zone 1 Senatorial District: Sabon Gari, Makarfi, Kudan, Sob

item.title: How political vanity is choking Nigeria’s future, By Adeola Akinremi
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “Democracy dies in darkness.” I thought about those words this week as I walked past The Washington Post headquarters on K Street in northwest, downtown Washington D.C. Some phrases become so familiar that they risk fading into the background. Then a particular moment restores their weight, remindin

item.title: Wenger admits World Cup hydration breaks split opinion
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Arsene Wenger said Saturday he accepted that the hydration breaks used at this year's World Cup were not universally popular and said FIFA will assess whether to implement them in the same way in the future. The post Wenger admits World Cup hydration breaks split opinion appeared first on Vanguard N

item.title: Heavy Rains: Move to safer areas now, NEMA tells Nigerians
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the statement, "The current period of heightened rainfall calls for the activation of community-level flood mitigation measures to minimise the impact of flooding."

item.title: Palestinian teenage footballer dies a week after Israeli settler attack
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A week after he was shot in his West Bank village, Fadi al-Nassan's death comes amid a surge in settler violence.

item.title: Sanwo-Olu: Merit, Not Ethnicity, Drives Appointments In Lagos
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Honours Deputy Chief of Staff, Egube, at Urhobo, Isoko reception Lagos State Governor, Mr Babajide Sanwo-Olu, has said appointments into his administration are guided by competence, integrity and excellence rather than ethnic considerations. Governor Sanwo-Olu stated this on Saturday at a rec

item.title: Gov Inuwa swears in new commissioners, others

item.description: Gombe State Governor, Muhammadu Inuwa Yahaya, on Saturday, presided over the swearing-in ceremony of the newly appointed Head of The post Gov Inuwa swears in new commissioners, others appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Three die, four injured in lone truck accident in Anambra – FRSC
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: “The crash involved seven male occupants. Three persons died, while four others sustained serious injuries,” she said. The post Three die, four injured in lone truck accident in Anambra – FRSC appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: China and global governance: An outsider’s perspectives
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The first thing that comes to mind when anyone thinks about China is its scale; scale of ambition, scale of achievement and scale of influence. Few countries have transformed themselves as dramatically or shaped global affairs as profoundly in the past four decades. That reality struck me again when

item.title: Drone video shows scale of major wildfire in northern Spain
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Drone video shows the scale of a major wildfire in northern Spain where more than 150 square kilometres have burned

item.title: Sanwo-Olu: Excellence, not ethnicity, earned my Urhobo Deputy Chief of Staff top role
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: "We don't ask where excellence comes from. What matters is the ability to do the job well and serve the people faithfully," he said.

item.title: Gang members shoot policeman while gunning for rivals linked to NURTW leaders
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "We did not know there were policemen in the area. It was Temidayo who fired the gun, while I was hit by bullets fired by the policemen" The post Gang members shoot policeman while gunning for rivals linked to NURTW leaders appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: World Cup final: Weather forces Spain to call off training but Argentina proceed
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: It was contrasting fortunes for the 206 FIFA World Cup finalists Spain and Argentina as weather disrupted training for both Spain and Argentina in New York on the eve of the biggest match in world football. Spain called off their outdoor training session due to threat of thunderstorm following a rai

item.title: Senator Katung Appeals For Calm Over Mary Habila’s Death
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Katung also called on the public to respect the family's desire to mourn privately and bury their daughter with dignity. The post Senator Katung Appeals For Calm Over Mary Habila’s Death appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Troops dismantle suspected illegal bunkering site in Lagos, arrest six
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of 65 Battalion have dismantled a suspected illegal bunkering site in the Orile General Area of Lagos State, arrested six suspects and recovered items linked to the illicit activity. Lieutenant Colonel Musa Yahaya, Acting Deputy Director Army Public Relations, 81 Division Nigerian Army, said

item.title: Khamenei threatens ‘unforgettable lessons’, two US troops killed in Iranian attack
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Majotaba Khamenei on Saturday vowed to teach the US "unforgettable lessons" in a written message broadcast on state TV. US Central Command reported its first troop deaths due to Iranian fire when two service members were killed and an another missing in an attack on

item.title: Chelsea hijack Arsenal bid to seal British Record £117m Morgan Rogers deal
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Rogers has already agreed personal terms on a six-year contract running until June 2032, with the option of a further year. A medical is scheduled for Monday.
